openQuestions.question: Can OpenCode `tool.execute.before` reject a Task call directly, or only mutate args?
How often does Phase-0/advisory-gate literalism occur across all deep modes and models?

resolvedQuestions.answer: Confirmed first-hand by operator; route-proof evidence is no longer required to acknowledge the behavioral problem.
Phase 0 general-agent self-assessment is a concrete Mode-D mechanism and already fired in phase 005 research smoke.
The ai-council route-proof issue is a false-pass risk against noncanonical identity, not just naming drift.
NDP-safe fix is registry reuse inside orchestrate plus direct leaf dispatch, not a depth-1 Task dispatch to primary @deep.

keyFindings.title: gpt-fast-high softened confirmed behavioral failures into missing route-proof artifacts
Phase 0 self-assessment gates are a literal-model trap across 8 deep commands
ai-council route-proof currently validates noncanonical council identity
orchestrate should reuse registry resolution, not Task-dispatch a primary @deep worker
plugin guard is feasible as mutation/injection; fail-closed and semantic correctness remain limits

ruledOutDirections.reason: Operator-confirmed symptoms are ground truth for this round.
Current route-proof path can validate noncanonical values and contaminate benchmark interpretation.

ruledOutDirections.approach: Treat plugin guard as hard identity
ruledOutDirections.reason: Available hook surface can inject/mutate prompt or tool args; it does not bind runtime custom-agent identity.

ruledOutDirections.approach: Immediate FIX-5 solely because symptoms are confirmed
ruledOutDirections.reason: FIX-5 does not target Phase-0 literalism or generic latency; use conditional unpark after cheap targeted fixes unless post-fix failures persist.
